signature_grapes.name: Garnacha Tinta
signature_grapes.description: Known internationally as Grenache, Garnacha Tinta is the dominant variety of DOQ Priorat, prized above all as old head-trained vines on llicorella slate. The extreme drainage stress and deep root penetration that llicorella forces produce naturally low yields and wines of intense red-fruit concentration, garrigue character and distinctive slate-driven minerality. Old-vine Garnacha in Priorat often exceeds 60, 80 or even 100 years of age, the oldest parcels producing the Gran Vinya Classificada wines from L'Ermita and Mas de la Rosa. The variety ripens mid-season and is sensitive to drought and wind; the costers terraces of the Priorat valleys create the sheltered micro-environments it needs.

signature_grapes.name: Cariñena (Samsó)
signature_grapes.description: Known as Carignan in French, Mazuelo in Rioja and Samsó in Catalan, Cariñena is the prestige old-vine variety of the Priorat, particularly the Cariñena heartland of Porrera. Late-ripening and naturally high in both tannin and acidity, it was once widely abandoned for its low yields but has been dramatically revalued in Priorat. Old-vine Cariñena on llicorella produces wines of dark fruit, wild herb and iron mineral character with exceptional ageing potential. The Mas de la Rosa Gran Vinya Classificada (Vall Llach) is predominantly Cariñena, as are several Vi de Paratge cuvees from Cims de Porrera and Sangenis i Vaque.

signature_grapes.name: Cabernet Sauvignon
signature_grapes.description: An authorised international variety in DOQ Priorat, where it was introduced by the founders of the modern era in the 1980s as a blending component. Cabernet Sauvignon adds structural tannin, blackcurrant fruit and ageing backbone to Garnacha-dominated blends. Its share in Priorat blends has generally declined since the early 2000s as producers focus on native Garnacha and Cariñena, but it remains present in many multi-varietal cuvees, including Clos de l'Obac and early Alvaro Palacios and Clos Mogador bottlings.

signature_grapes.name: Syrah
signature_grapes.description: An authorised variety in DOQ Priorat, where it contributes dark fruit, violet aromatics, spice and mid-palate richness to blends. Syrah ripens relatively easily in the hot Priorat summers and is used by producers including Clos de l'Obac and Ferrer Bobet as a blending component. It is rarely bottled as a varietal in Priorat; its role is primarily structural and aromatic complementation to the native Garnacha.

signature_grapes.name: Garnacha Blanca
signature_grapes.description: The principal white variety of DOQ Priorat, and the foundation of the region's small but growing white wine category. Garnacha Blanca produces full-bodied, low-acid wines with ripe stone-fruit, almond and herbal character on llicorella soils. When handled with careful temperature control and early picking, it develops a vivid mineral edge. Clos de l'Obac's Kyrie and Terroir al Limit's Pedra de Guix are notable Priorat whites based on this grape.

signature_grapes.name: Macabeo (Viura)
signature_grapes.description: Also known as Viura in Rioja, Macabeo is the most widely planted white grape in Catalonia and an authorised variety in DOQ Priorat. It is used primarily as a blending component in white cuvees alongside Garnacha Blanca, contributing fresh acidity and citrus aromatics. The Vinicola del Priorat cooperative uses Macabeo in its Onix Clasic Blanc, and several estates blend it into their white wines to balance Garnacha Blanca's fuller body.

signature_grapes.name: Pedro Ximenez
signature_grapes.description: An authorised white variety in DOQ Priorat, used by Terroir al Limit for its Pedra de Guix Vi de Coster cuvee in dry-vinified form. Unlike its role in Montilla-Moriles and Jerez (where it makes sweet, raisined wines), Priorat's Pedro Ximenez is harvested early and fermented dry, yielding wines of surprising freshness and mineral acidity on llicorella soils. The variety's natural high-sugar potential is tamed by Dominik Huber's early-picking approach.

signature_grapes.name: Merlot
signature_grapes.description: An authorised blending variety in DOQ Priorat, used by some estates including Clos de l'Obac and Mas Martinet in their multi-varietal cuvees. Merlot contributes softness, plum fruit and mid-palate flesh to blends, and was more widely used in early-era Priorat wines when producers were establishing their house styles. Its presence in current blends is generally smaller than in the 1990s, as the regional trend has moved toward Garnacha and Cariñena purity.
